publication/title: Intelligent prediction of RBC demand in trauma patients using decision tree methods
publication/authors: Feng, Y-N; Xu, Z-H; Liu, J-T; Sun, X-L; Wang, D-Q; Yu, Y

dataset/provenance:
* **Data Source Name**:
  * Emergency Trauma Database of the First Medical Center of Chinese PLA General Hospital.
  * Clinical blood transfusion intelligent management and evaluation system database.

* **Total Data Points**:
  * N=22,491 (critically ill patients).
  * N=1,371 (trauma patients included in the study).

* **Previous Use**:
  * No. Not enough information is available.
dataset/splits:

  * Test: 275 (20% of 1371)

  * Test: Not enough information is available.
dataset/redundancy:
* **Redundancy Methodology**: The historical dataset was randomly divided into an 80% training set and a 20% test set. The model was trained on the training set, and the effect of the model was evaluated on the test set.

* **Independent Sets**: Yes. The dataset was randomly split into training and test sets, which suggests an attempt to ensure independence. However, there is no explicit mention of steps taken to prevent leakage beyond random splitting.

* **Independence Enforcement**: Not enough information is available.

optimization/encoding:
* **Data Encoding Method: Numerical Vector Conversion**
  The variables were converted into numerical vectors for model building. This includes categorical variables such as gender.

* **Data Encoding Method: Natural Language Processing (NLP)**
  Unstructured text variables were processed using an automatic counting word segmentation algorithm in NLP to transform words into numerical variables.

* **Data Encoding Method: New Variable Construction**
  Diagnostic information of patients, such as diagnostic details and trauma location, were divided into phrases and fields. Different categories in the target variables were counted and scored to construct new variables. The model was then trained using the learned rules to create these new variables.
optimization/parameters:
* **Parameter Count (p)**:
  * Not enough information is available.

* **Architecture Selection**:
  * Architecture Selection: The authors did not explicitly disclose the method used for architecture selection. The models used were Logistic Regression (LR), Classification and Regression Tree (CRT), and eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost). The choice of these models appears to be based on their suitability for the tasks of classification and regression, with no specific optimization technique mentioned for selecting the architecture.
optimization/features:

* Training-Restricted Selection: Unclear
optimization/fitting:
* **p > N: No**

  The training set size (N) is 1,096 (80% of 1,371 patients). The XGBoost algorithm is used, which is an ensemble of decision trees. The exact number of parameters (p) in an XGBoost model depends on the number of trees and their depths, but it is generally much larger than the number of features. However, the regularization function in XGBoost helps to control the complexity of the model and prevent overfitting, even when p > N.

* **Over-fitting Evidence**: Regularization function in XGBoost, model evaluation on a separate test set (20% of data), comparison of AUC results using t-test method provided by SciPy library in Python software.
optimization/regularization:
Regularization Techniques:
* XGBoost: The regular function is used to detect the complexity of the model to avoid overfitting.

evaluation/method:
* **Evaluation Strategy**: Independent hold-out sets.
  The dataset was randomly divided into an 80% training set and a 20% test set. The model was trained on the training set, and the effect of the model was evaluated on the test set.
evaluation/measure:
**Performance Metrics:**

* **Method 1 (Non-invasive parameters):**
  * AUC: 0.72 (LR), 0.71 (XGBoost), 0.69 (CRT)
  * Accuracy: 0.55 (LR), 0.75 (XGBoost), 0.48 (CRT)
  * Sensitivity: 0.86 (LR), 0.66 (XGBoost), 0.89 (CRT)
  * Specificity: 0.50 (LR), 0.77 (XGBoost), 0.42 (CRT)
  * Youden Index: 0.12 (LR), 0.19 (XGBoost), 0.16 (CRT)

* **Method 2 (All parameters):**
  * AUC: 0.80 (LR), 0.94 (XGBoost), 0.82 (CRT)
  * Accuracy: 0.72 (LR), 0.83 (XGBoost), 0.89 (CRT)
  * Sensitivity: 0.80 (LR), 0.94 (XGBoost), 0.69 (CRT)
  * Specificity: 0.70 (LR), 0.82 (XGBoost), 0.92 (CRT)
  * Youden Index: 0.12 (LR), 0.10 (XGBoost), 0.09 (CRT)
